title: Lesson 403
transcript: Hello. A bit of terminology that you might come across here in the industry or on the exam related to the location of where you're conducting your audit. Most of this is pretty self-explanatory. So on-premise audits obviously focus on systems, auditing, testing, assessing systems that are on-premise. Systems that are in the company's, the organization's own data center within their realm. That's an on-premise audit. cloud-based audits is exactly what you think so what you think it is a cloud-based audit focuses on systems that are in the cloud sas systems infrastructures of service systems you know cloud audit focuses on those the one that's worth spending just a moment on is you might come across the term of a hybrid audit and what exactly is that focusing on Hybrid audits tend to focus on the connectivity between on-premise and the cloud. Because what happens all the time nowadays, right, is organizations, they'll have on-premise systems. And for instance, they might have their Active Directory system. on-premise and the Active Directory system acts as the identity store for their federated identity management. And then the way that their users are connecting to cloud-based systems is through a federated management protocol like SAML or something like that. And so what you'd be looking at in a hybrid audit is how is that connection working? How is it that the user is authenticating to these cloud-based services using say the on-prem federated, you know, the on prem identity provider, or you might be another big part of hybrid audits is you're looking at how the data flows between on prem systems and cloud based systems. How is that data transitioned securely? What data is passing back and forth? what should be allowed to, if data is being passed back and forth, are you achieving the right confidentiality, the integrity of that data? How do you assure availability of data that might be in the cloud or on-prem or moving it back and forth? So hybrid audits, the primary focus of them and the key words to remember here is the connectivity between on prem and cloud based systems. So it's the authentication movement of data, etc. you
